data.change_summary: Tightened milestone task-run lineage in the two remaining audit gaps. `matching_finalized_task_runs` now only falls back from a requested `run_id` to runless terminal rows with the same `started_at`, so a finalized named run no longer blocks a different named retry that happens to share a timestamp. Completion journal details now carry `started_at`, and the filesystem journal-repair path only merges/replaces completion events when both rows identify the same attempt, which prevents same-timestamp multi-attempt completions from being collapsed into one journal entry. Added model and service regressions for both behaviors and updated completion-detail expectations to the new structured payload.
outstanding_risks: Milestone mutations still span separate lineage, snapshot, and journal files without a cross-file transaction, so a crash between file writes can still leave snapshot or journal state temporarily behind `task-runs.ndjson` until the same operation is replayed or state is reconciled from lineage.

Manually corrupted lineage that already contains multiple simultaneous open attempts for the same bead still rejects new starts or runless completions as ambiguous until the bad state is repaired or richer caller metadata is supplied.

steps.description: Narrowed finalized-attempt matching in `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs` so a requested named run only falls back to runless terminal rows, and added a regression covering same-timestamp distinct named runs.
Extended completion journal detail rendering/parsing to include `started_at`, then tightened `FsMilestoneJournalStore` repair merging so only the same attempt can be repaired in place.

Added milestone_record service regressions proving same-timestamp completion events for different attempts stay separate and that a distinct named retry can start even when another named attempt finalized at the same `started_at`.
Reformatted the workspace and revalidated the milestone_record path after the changes.
